1258099.name: Ice Chunk
1258099.summary: Drops ice/ore impacts that create blocking objects and bad ground.

advice.generic: Ice Chunk/ore-style impacts create objects or bad ground; avoid the impact and keep usable cover/space intact.
advice.tank: Coordinate which chunk is preserved for boss mechanics.
advice.healer: Watch players who get clipped or stand far from cover.
advice.dps: Do not destroy or drop mechanics on chunks the group still needs.

1258434.name: Curse of Torment

advice.generic: Curse of Torment is a curse with a healing absorb and Shadow DoT; remove it if curse dispel is available, otherwise heal the absorb off.
advice.healer: Call for curse dispel if your class cannot remove it; commit healing if no curse removal is available.
advice.dps: Use curse removal on yourself/allies when possible. This saves healer throughput.

1258434.notes: Curse with a healing absorb + Shadow DoT; remove promptly where a curse dispel is available.

1258437.name: Permeating Cold
1258437.summary: A Magic frost DoT with a heavy slow; dispel or clear with movement utility.

advice.generic: Permeating Cold is a Magic frost DoT/slow; dispel it or clear with freedom/snare-removal utility.
advice.healer: Dispel one target quickly and call for freedom/self-clears on the other if multiple are active.
advice.dps: Use snare clear/freedom-style utility if you have it; do not sit slowed in ground mechanics.

1258437.notes: Magic frost DoT + heavy slow; dispel OR clear with movement utility (snare purge clears the whole aura).

1258459.name: Rotting Strikes

advice.generic: Rotting Strikes stacks reduce max health; disease dispel early or the tank becomes much easier to kill.
advice.tank: Watch stacks and kite if disease removal is unavailable or delayed.
advice.healer: Disease dispel the tank before stacks make normal hits lethal.
advice.dps: Use disease removal/off-utility if available and help control Rotlings.

1258459.notes: Discovered from logs (cleared by Burn It Out). Stacking max-health-reduction disease; dispel.

advice.generic: Leaping Maul cleaves around/near the target; loosely spread so one leap does not hit multiple players.
advice.healer: Spot heal the leap target if it overlaps other physical damage.
advice.dps: Do not stack tightly on trash with leaping geists active.

1258997.name: Plungegrip

advice.generic: Plungegrip is physical control handled by interrupt/freedom, not healer cleansing.
advice.tank: Position so a gripped player is not dragged through lethal ground.
advice.healer: Heal the target and ask for freedom/interrupt rather than spending cleanse globals.
advice.dps: Stop the channel or free the target with utility.

1258997.notes: Physical grip channel; interrupt or Freedom-style utility, not a healer cleanse.

advice.generic: Blight Splatter leaves puddles; avoid the puddles and soothe Plague Frenzy if available to reduce pressure.
advice.tank: Move the mob out of puddles and call for soothe during enrage.
advice.healer: Avoid standing in splatter while healing through other plague damage.
advice.dps: Soothe enrage if possible and keep damage out of bad ground.

advice.generic: Cryoburst targets all players; loosely spread to avoid cleaving each other, then move as a group away from the puddles spawned.
advice.tank: Move the mob/boss so puddles do not trap the group in the next pattern.
advice.healer: Top players before the group spread and stabilize as they move away.
advice.dps: Spread enough to avoid overlaps, then regroup after puddles are down.

advice.generic: Throw Saronite targets two players; keep circles separated and avoid overlapping Ore Chunks, because destroyed chunks leave large area-denial puddles.
advice.tank: Plan which Ore Chunk you will later destroy with Orebreaker and keep one available for Glacial Overload line-of-sight.
advice.healer: Watch targeted players and remind the group not to destroy all cover before Glacial Overload.
advice.dps: Place your Saronite circle cleanly; do not overlap another target or delete needed cover.

advice.generic: Orebreaker targets the tank; overlap one Ore Chunk to break it and avoid the stun, while preserving another chunk for Glacial Overload.
advice.tank: Use a defensive and deliberately break the assigned Ore Chunk; do not accidentally destroy the last cover.
advice.healer: Tank will spike during Orebreaker; be ready especially if chunk positioning is messy.
advice.dps: Stay clear of the tank/chunk impact and preserve remaining cover.

1261921.name: Cryoshards

advice.generic: Cryoshards is a Magic snare/damage effect; dispel or clear with movement utility before it causes a failed movement check.
advice.healer: Prioritize targets who need to move for Glacial Overload or dodge patterns.
advice.dps: Self-clear if possible and avoid using mobility into the snare without removing it.

1261921.notes: Discovered from logs (cleared by Cleanse/Detox). DB2: Magic + snare; dispel or clear with movement utility.

advice.generic: Glacial Overload requires line of sight behind a remaining Ore Chunk; hide before the channel finishes.
advice.tank: Keep at least one Ore Chunk alive and position the boss/path so everyone can reach it.
advice.healer: Top the group before the channel and dispel Cryostomp/Permeating Cold effects afterward.
advice.dps: Stop tunneling and get behind the chunk early; late movement wipes groups.

advice.generic: Scourgelord’s Brand knocks the tank back, then leaps to that position; tank uses a defensive and moves to avoid the follow-up leap.
advice.tank: Pre-position so the knockback is safe, use a defensive, then move immediately before the leap lands.
advice.healer: Track the tank through the knockback/leap sequence and be ready for range/line issues.
advice.dps: Stay out of the tank’s knockback/leap landing zone.

advice.generic: Rime Blast targets should aim/position to cleave an infused Bone Pile marked by green light, controlling which add spawns later.
advice.tank: Plan where the boss faces/stands so Rime Blast targets can hit the intended Bone Pile safely.
advice.healer: Be ready for players moving into position for the Bone Pile cleave.
advice.dps: If targeted, line the blast through the marked Bone Pile; otherwise avoid the line.

1262930.name: Rotting Strikes

advice.generic: Rotting Strikes is stacking max-health reduction; remove disease stacks before they snowball.
advice.tank: Call stack count and use defensives/kiting while waiting for cleanse.
advice.healer: Cleanse promptly; do not let stacks sit through a tank buster.
advice.dps: Control or burst Rotlings to limit new applications.

1262930.notes: Discovered from logs. Stacking max-health-reduction disease; dispel.

advice.generic: Death’s Grasp creates dodge circles during Tyrannus; move out quickly and keep paths open while handling adds.
advice.tank: Do not tank adds where Death’s Grasp circles cut off the group.
advice.healer: Avoid standing in predictable circle clusters while healing the add wave.
advice.dps: Move first, then resume interrupts/cleave on Plaguespreaders.

advice.generic: Blight Smash is a tank hit that drops a puddle; tank uses a defensive and places the puddle out of the way.
advice.tank: Drop the puddle at the edge or in old bad space, not under the boss path.
advice.healer: Expect tank damage plus group puddle movement immediately after.
advice.dps: Do not stand behind/near the tank drop location.

advice.generic: Plague Expulsion/Explosion creates multiple puddles under players; pre-position along edges and use personals if low.
advice.tank: Keep bosses/adds positioned so players can spread without losing all cleave.
advice.healer: Prepare for group movement and spot healing as four puddles appear.
advice.dps: Place your puddle at the edge or on existing bad if safe; do not fill the center.

advice.generic: During Get 'Em, Ick!, the boss fixates a random player for several seconds; targeted player kites, everyone else maximizes cleave safely.
advice.tank: Be ready to regain control after the fixate and keep the shared-health bosses cleavable.
advice.healer: Watch the fixated player and use externals if they are cornered.
advice.dps: If targeted, kite through safe space; if not targeted, save damage windows for boss cleave.

advice.generic: Icy Blast is a hard-hitting tank cast; interrupt it consistently.
advice.tank: Use a defensive if a cast is missed; it hits extremely hard.
advice.healer: Be ready for tank spikes if interrupts fail.
advice.dps: Assign kicks; this is a real priority cast, not filler.

advice.generic: Netherburst is a large AoE cast and should be interrupted every time.
advice.healer: Prepare group healing only if a cast slips.
advice.dps: Hold interrupts for Netherburst when Arcanist Cadavers are active.

advice.generic: Ore Chunks are both cover and future puddles; preserve at least one for Glacial Overload and avoid destroying chunks in the middle of the room.
advice.tank: Assign chunk usage for Orebreaker and keep the remaining chunk accessible to the whole group.
advice.dps: Do not cleave or overlap mechanics into chunks the group still needs for cover.

advice.generic: Ice Barrage channels several circles; keep moving and do not overlap pathing with other players.
advice.healer: Watch players who are dodging while diseased/low.
advice.dps: Maintain interrupts on Plaguespreaders while dodging barrage circles.

advice.generic: Dark Rupture creates circles; step out and keep mobs out of the puddled area afterward.
advice.tank: Move the pack after Dark Rupture if the circle lands in melee space.
advice.healer: Spot heal late movers and prepare for tank hits in the same trash pulls.
advice.dps: Do not finish a cast inside Dark Rupture; sidestep and continue.

advice.generic: Frost Breath is a cone/frontal aimed at a player; sidestep the cone and avoid standing in front of the drake.
advice.tank: Face the drake away from the party when possible and be ready for frost-enhanced melees.
advice.healer: Watch random targets if the cone clips them during other trash damage.
advice.dps: Do not stand in front; if targeted, move out of the cone path.
